response:
solution the core error in the provided erroneous code lies in failing to handle duplicate values by not separating the median elements from the rest of the array before reordering.
solution a robust approach involves sorting the array first and then using a virtual index mapping to interleave elements from the second half with the first half.
solution this virtual index strategy ensures that equal elements are pushed far enough apart to satisfy the wiggle condition even when duplicates exist.
solution for the follow-up question regarding O(n) time complexity, one can use the Quickselect algorithm to find the median in linear time instead of full sorting.
solution once the median is identified, a partitioning step similar to Quickselect can be used to place elements relative to the median without full sorting.
solution the same virtual index mapping technique applies after this linear partitioning to achieve the final correct order.
solution this method naturally extends to negative integers because the comparison logic relies only on relative magnitude which holds true for the entire integer range.
solution the final output will correctly satisfy the wiggle sort requirement for all valid inputs including those with many duplicates.
